lumbar_esi.name: Lumbar Epidural Steroid Injection
lumbar_esi.source: CMS LCD L36920 / Medicare Coverage Policy
criteria: Diagnosis of radiculopathy, radicular pain, or neurogenic claudication
Confirmation by imaging (MRI, CT, or myelogram) showing nerve root compression or spinal stenosis
Conservative treatment attempted for minimum 6 weeks (physical therapy AND/OR anti-inflammatory medications)
Functional impairment documented (pain score, activity limitations, work limitations)
No absolute contraindications (active infection, bleeding disorder, allergy to steroids or contrast)
No evidence of medical emergency requiring immediate surgery (severe neurological deficit, cauda equina syndrome)

sleep_study.name: Polysomnography (Sleep Study)
criteria: Clinical symptoms of sleep-disordered breathing: excessive daytime sleepiness, witnessed apneas, loud snoring, or nocturnal choking
Epworth Sleepiness Scale score ≥10 OR documented witnessed apneas OR STOP-BANG score ≥3
BMI >30 OR neck circumference >40cm (male) / >38cm (female) OR other OSA risk factors OR clinical necessity documented
No prior definitive sleep study within 12 months unless clinical change documented
Ordering physician documents medical necessity and expected clinical impact on management

cardiac_stress.name: Cardiac Stress Test (Exercise or Pharmacologic)
criteria: Signs or symptoms suggestive of coronary artery disease (chest pain, dyspnea, syncope, palpitations with exertion)
Known CAD with new or changing symptoms OR post-revascularization follow-up with symptoms
Pre-operative risk stratification for high/intermediate risk surgery with poor or unknown functional capacity
Intermediate pre-test probability of CAD based on age, sex, and symptom characteristics
Resting ECG interpretable (or pharmacologic stress selected if LBBB/pacemaker/digoxin changes present)
Not indicated for routine screening in asymptomatic low-risk patients

knee_arthroscopy.name: Knee Arthroscopy
criteria: MRI or clinical evidence of mechanical pathology (meniscal tear, loose body, osteochondral lesion, ACL/PCL tear with instability)
Mechanical symptoms present: locking, catching, giving way (not just pain alone)
Conservative treatment for minimum 6 weeks (physical therapy, NSAIDs, activity modification) — except for acute instability/locked knee
Functional impairment affecting activities of daily living or occupation
NOT indicated for isolated knee osteoarthritis without mechanical symptoms (per AAOS guidelines)
Pre-operative imaging reviewed and documented in clinical notes

mri_brain.name: MRI Brain with Contrast
criteria: New onset neurological symptoms: seizures, focal deficits, sudden severe headache, vision changes, cognitive decline
Known or suspected CNS pathology requiring characterization (tumor, infection, MS, vascular malformation)
Prior non-contrast MRI or CT showing abnormality requiring further characterization
Contrast indicated specifically (not just routine) — active infection, malignancy workup, BBB breakdown assessment
Clinical scenario where MRI findings will change management
Repeat imaging within 12 months requires documented clinical change or new indication
